If you’re about to melt down in public.

---

Do now (5 mins):

Carry kit: earplugs, sunglasses, stim.

Title: Sensory overload: The meltdown you can't control

Author: SOR7ED
CTA: Lost your job? Text JOBLOSS to +44 7966 628285 for benefits support and ND-friendly job search.
Excerpt: You've been fired. Again. Not for incompetence—for being late, forgetting meetings, or 'not fitting in.' Here's the survival protocol.
Meta Description: Job loss can trigger shock, shame, and shutdown. Learn what to do in the first 72 hours, from benefits to routines that stabilize.
SEO Title: Job Loss Survival Guide: What to Do in the First 72 Hours
Template:
💼 72-Hour Career Crisis Plan

Just lost your job and spiralling? No career-coach BS. Just immediate survival steps.

---

### IF YOU'RE IN CRISIS RIGHT NOW
Text someone: "I just got fired. I need to hear I’ll survive this."
If suicidal: Samaritans 116 123

---

### HOUR 1–24: IMMEDIATE SURVIVAL
- Eat something
- Sleep
- Don’t panic-apply
- Check your contract for notice pay and holiday pay

---

### HOUR 24–48: SECURE THE BASICS
- Apply for Universal Credit today: http://gov.uk/universal-credit
- Check termination letter and final pay
- Request a neutral reference

---

### HOUR 48–72: DAMAGE CONTROL
- List monthly expenses
- Contact landlord early
- Cancel non-essential subscriptions
- Write your 2-sentence interview story

---

ACAS (workplace rights): 0300 123 1100

— SOR7ED
Title: Job loss and unemployment: The career collapse nobody saw coming

Author: SOR7ED
CTA: Alcohol and meds crisis? Text ALCOHOL to https://wa.me/447360277713?text=ALCOHOL for harm reduction and safety protocols.
Excerpt: Mixing stimulants and alcohol isn't just 'bad for you.' It masks dangerous signals and can lead to alcohol poisoning. Here's what actually happens.
Meta Description: Mixing ADHD stimulants with alcohol can hide danger signs and increase risk. Here’s what happens in your body and how to stay safer.
SEO Title: Alcohol and ADHD Meds: Why Mixing Them Is Dangerous
Template:
🍺 Alcohol + ADHD Meds Harm-Reduction Checklist

Concerned about your drinking patterns? Here’s the truth about mixing stimulants and alcohol.

---

### IF YOU'RE IN DANGER RIGHT NOW
☎️ Call 999 or go to A&E if you have withdrawal symptoms, alcohol poisoning risk, chest pain, or blackouts.
**Drinkline:** 0300 123 1110

---

### WHY THIS HAPPENS
1) Stimulants (Elvanse, Concerta, Ritalin, Adderall) mask alcohol’s effects
2) You drink more because you don’t feel drunk
3) When meds wear off, alcohol hits all at once
4) Heart and liver strain increases

---

### HARM REDUCTION (If not ready to quit)
**1. Timing:** Don’t drink while meds are active (Elvanse 12+ hrs).
**2. Limits:** Max 2 drinks total. Sip slowly. Alternate water.
**3. Never alone:** Friend knows you’re medicated and can monitor.
**4. Red flags:** Blackout, racing heart, chest pain → 999.

---

### ALTERNATIVE REGULATION MENU
Instead of alcohol to come down:
- 20-minute walk
- Hot shower
- Weighted blanket
- Magnesium glycinate
- Chamomile/valerian tea

---

### UK RESOURCES
- Drinkline: 0300 123 1110
- AA: 0800 9177 650
- SMART Recovery UK: http://smartrecovery.org.uk
- Club Soda: http://joinclubsoda.com

— SOR7ED
Title: Alcohol and ADHD meds: The dangerous interaction

Template:
🧠 PARENTS: Anger + boundaries (without re-traumatising)

If repair feels like walking back into trauma, treat it like a data audit, not emotional reconciliation.

---

Make a simple timeline of triggering events. Write the facts, the action, and your reaction.

Name the theme you keep seeing, like control, dismissal, or shame. One sentence is enough.

Set one small boundary based on the theme. Example: “I won’t discuss my diagnosis if you’re going to debate it.” Practice saying no and ending the conversation.

Read full: https://sor7ed.com/blog/Anger%20at%20Parents%2C%20Even%20Now%3A%20How%20to%20Repair%20Without%20Re-Traumatizing%20Yourself

— SOR7ED
Title: Anger at parents: Repair without re-traumatizing yourself

CTA: Burnout or depression? Text BURNOUT to https://wa.me/447360277713?text=BURNOUT for rest protocols and assessment support.

SEO Title: Autistic Burnout vs Depression: How to Tell and What Helps
Template:
🔥 BURNOUT: Autistic burnout vs depression (quick sort)

If the usual advice makes you worse, check burnout. Burnout needs demand reduction and sensory protection, not “try harder.”

---

Do a sensory audit. Identify one trigger and make one tiny fix.

Build an energy budget. Block 15 minutes of unstructured rest with zero expectations.

Tell one trusted person one unmet need. Example: “Please don’t ask me about X for a while. I’m at capacity.”

Author: SOR7ED
CTA: Hyperfocus ruining your health? Text HYPERFOCUS to +44 7966 628285 for break reminders and boundaries.
Excerpt: You can code for 14 hours straight. But you can't remember to eat, sleep, or pee. Here's how to harness hyperfocus without burning out.
Meta Description: Hyperfocus can feel like a superpower until it breaks sleep, food, and relationships. Learn how to harness it without burnout.
SEO Title: ADHD Hyperfocus: The Superpower That Can Ruin Your Life
Template:
🧠 Hyperfocus Survival Kit

Lost in the tunnel and forgot to eat/sleep/exist? Here's your complete hyperfocus management system.

---

### IF YOU'RE IN THE TUNNEL RIGHT NOW
**Get out in 5 minutes:**
1. **Stand up** (breaks the physical state)
2. **Leave the room** (gets you out of environment)
3. **Drink water** (rehydrate)
4. **Eat something** (you forgot, didn't you?)
5. **Move your body** (walk, stretch, anything)
DO NOT "just finish this one thing" (you won't stop).

---

### ENTER RITUALS (Make hyperfocus more likely)
**Environmental setup:**
- Same physical space
- Same coffee/drink
- Same playlist
- Remove ALL distractions (phone in other room)
- Close unnecessary tabs/apps
**Mental prep:**
- "I'm going into the tunnel for [X hours]"
- Set boundaries: "I'll be unreachable until [time]"
- Warn people in advance
**Novelty triggers:**
- New notebook for this project
- New problem to solve
- New angle on familiar topic

---

### EXIT PROTOCOLS (Get out before the crash)
**Set multiple alarms:**
- **Hour 2:** "Stand up and stretch" (don't ignore this)
- **Hour 4:** "Eat something" (seriously, eat)
- **Hour 6:** "HARD STOP" (must leave room)
**Best exit method: HUMAN INTERRUPT**
Ask someone to:
- Call you at [time]
- Don't let you hang up until you stand up
- Physically come get you if you don't respond
**When alarm goes off:**
✓ Stand immediately
✓ Leave the room
✗ "Just one more thing" (LIE)
✗ Check phone first (new tunnel)

---

### RECOVERY CHECKLIST (Post-hyperfocus hangover)
You just ran a mental marathon. Your brain is WRECKED.
**Immediate (0-2 hours):**
- Eat protein (brain recovery fuel)
- Drink 2+ glasses of water (you're dehydrated)
- Lie down if possible
- Do LOW-DEMAND activity (comfort show, easy game)
**Avoid for 4+ hours:**
✗ Big decisions (you're impaired)
✗ Difficult conversations (you're fragile)
✗ Starting another hyperfocus (back-to-back = crash)
**Expect:**
- Brain fog
- Exhaustion
- Headache
- Emotional fragility (crying at commercials)
- Delayed hunger (eat anyway)

---

### PARTNER COMMUNICATION SCRIPTS
**Before you start:**
"Hey, I'm going into the tunnel for [X hours]. I'll be unreachable. I still love you. See you at [time]."
**Set check-in alarm:**
Every 2-3 hours, text them: 👋
(Shows you're alive and thinking of them)
**When they interrupt you:**
Don't snap. Use script:
"I'm in hyperfocus. Give me 15 minutes to finish this section, then I'm all yours."
**After you emerge:**
"Sorry if I was distant. That was hyperfocus, not you. Thank you for being patient."
**Explain what it feels like:**
"It's like being underwater. I can see you waving, but I can't hear you. I can't come up for air until my brain is done."

---

### QUICK REFERENCE (Screenshot this)
**BEFORE:**
- Warn people
- Set alarms
- Remove distractions
- Have food/water nearby
**DURING:**
- Ignore everything except alarms
- When alarm rings: STAND UP
- Don't "just one more"
**AFTER:**
- Eat + drink
- Rest (no decisions)
- Thank people who waited
- Schedule recovery time

Full guide: http://sor7ed.com/hyperfocus-superpower-and-trap

— SOR7ED
Title: ADHD hyperfocus: The superpower that ruins your life

Author: SOR7ED

Aftercare script: “How are you feeling about what happened?”

Author: SOR7ED
CTA: Meltdown at work? Text MELTDOWN to +44 7966 628285 for P.A.R.K. protocol and exit strategies.
Excerpt: You're about to cry, scream, or shut down at your desk. Here's the emergency protocol for workplace meltdowns.
Meta Description: Workplace meltdowns are nervous system overload, not bad attitude. Learn an emergency exit plan and reset steps you can use fast.
SEO Title: Meltdowns at Work: Emergency Reset and Exit Strategies
Template:
🚨 Workplace Meltdown Protocol

About to meltdown at work and need an exit plan?
Not a tantrum. Not weakness. A nervous system in crisis.

---

### IF YOU'RE MELTING DOWN RIGHT NOW
**Emergency exit (use immediately):**
**Step 1: Leave the area**
- Bathroom (lock door)
- Outside (even 5 minutes)
- Quiet room
- Your car
**Step 2: Ground your body**
- Cold water on face + wrists
- Deep breaths: In 4, hold 4, out 6
- If you can, cry (releases cortisol)
**Step 3: Text someone**
"I'm melting down at work. I'm safe but need 15 minutes."
**DO NOT:**
✗ Try to power through (makes it worse)
✗ Apologize to coworkers yet (wait until calm)
✗ Make decisions (you're dysregulated)

---

### THE WORK P.A.R.K. PROTOCOL
**P - Pause**
- Stop what you're doing
- Don't try to finish "just this one thing"
**A - Acknowledge**
- "I'm overstimulated"
- "My nervous system is overwhelmed"
- Not "I'm being dramatic"
**R - Retreat**
- Leave the environment (bathroom, outside, quiet room)
- Remove yourself from sensory triggers
- Tell someone: "I need 15 minutes"
**K - Kindness**
- "This is my brain protecting me"
- "I'm not broken, I'm overwhelmed"
- Rest, don't shame

---

### MANAGER COMMUNICATION TEMPLATES
**Immediate (when you need to leave):**
**Email:**
"Hi [Manager], I need to step away for 15 minutes to manage sensory overload. I'll be back shortly and will catch up on anything I missed."
**Slack:**
"Overstimulated - taking 15 min break. Back soon 👍"
**In person:**
"I'm experiencing sensory overload and need a brief break. I'll be back in 15 minutes."

---

### UK WORKPLACE RIGHTS
Equality Act 2010 covers ADHD/Autism. Employers must provide reasonable adjustments.
If denied, document it and contact ACAS: 0300 123 1100.

Related: http://sor7ed.com/meltdowns-at-work-protocols

— SOR7ED
Title: Meltdowns at work: When your nervous system says no

Author: SOR7ED
CTA: Meltdown at work? Text PARK to +44 7966 628285 for the P.A.R.K. protocol and exit strategies.
Excerpt: You're about to melt down at work. Here's the 4-step protocol: Pause, Acknowledge, Reset, Keep Going.
Meta Description: About to melt down at work? Use the P.A.R.K. protocol to pause, acknowledge overload, reset your nervous system, and keep going.
SEO Title: P.A.R.K. Protocol: How to Stop a Meltdown at Work
Template:
🅿️ PARK: Work meltdown management (P.A.R.K.)

When you’re about to melt down at work but can’t.

---

P — Pause: stop. don’t push through.
A — Acknowledge: “I’m overstimulated.”
R — Reset: cold water, slow breath, leave area 5–15 mins.
K — Keep going (smaller): return to ONE tiny task only.

Script to manager:
“I need a 15-minute break for sensory overload. Back shortly.”

— SOR7ED
Title: Work meltdown management: The P.A.R.K. protocol

Author: SOR7ED
CTA: Drowning in debt? Text DEBT to https://wa.me/447360277713?text=DEBT for crisis support and payment plans.
Excerpt: Impulse purchases, forgotten bills, late fees. Now you're in thousands of debt. Here's the damage control protocol.
Meta Description: ADHD debt spirals come from impulse spending, avoidance, and late fees. Learn a step-by-step damage control plan to get unstuck.
SEO Title: ADHD Debt Spiral: Stop the Bleeding and Get Back on Track
Template:
💳 Debt Triage Protocol (Shame-Free)

Drowning in debt and can't face the numbers?
ADHD impulse spending isn't moral failure. It's dopamine regulation.

---

### IF YOU'RE IN IMMEDIATE CRISIS
StepChange: 0800 138 1111
National Debtline: 0808 808 4000

---

### STOP THE BLEEDING (Right Now)
1) Freeze the cards (literal ice block if needed)
2) Delete saved payment info (Amazon, Apple Pay, etc.)
3) Unsubscribe from marketing and block triggers

---

### FACE THE NUMBER (Once)
Total debt: £_
- Credit card 1: £_
- Credit card 2: £_
- Overdraft: £_
- Loans: £_
- BNPL: £_

---

### AUTOMATE EVERYTHING
- Minimum payments on every debt, scheduled right after payday
- Essential bills first: rent, utilities, food

---

### SPENDING PAUSE PROTOCOL (Before any purchase over £20)
1) Wait 24 hours
2) Ask: Do I need this? Will I use it in 30 days? Can I afford it without credit?
If any NO → don’t buy.

---

UK resources:
- StepChange: 0800 138 1111
- Citizens Advice: http://citizensadvice.org.uk

— SOR7ED
Title: Debt spiral: ADHD impulse spending and the way out

Author: SOR7ED
CTA: Want the fix? Text DECLUTTER to https://wa.me/447360277713?text=DECLUTTER for the ND-friendly declutter protocol.
Excerpt: You want to declutter. But every item has a memory. Every decision is exhausting. Here's the protocol that actually works.
Meta Description: Decluttering with ADHD can trigger spirals and shutdown. Use a simple protocol to start, make decisions faster, and stop overwhelm.
SEO Title: Decluttering with ADHD: A Simple Protocol to Start
TL;DR: Decluttering with ADHD isn't a motivation problem. It's a decision-fatigue and attention problem. One surface, a 10-minute timer, and three piles (Keep / Bin / Not Sure) is all you need to start without spiralling.
Template:
🧹 DECLUTTER: ADHD declutter starter protocol

If the closet opens and you spiral, do this instead.

STEP 1: PICK ONE SURFACE. NOT A ROOM.
A drawer. A corner of a shelf. The top of the bedside table. That's it. If you can't decide, pick the one that annoys you most when you look at it.

STEP 2: SET A TIMER FOR 10 MINUTES.
Not 30. Not "until it's done." Ten minutes. The timer is your exit ramp. Knowing it ends is what makes it possible to start.

STEP 3: MAKE ONLY THREE PILES.
KEEP. BIN. NOT SURE.
That's the entire decision tree. No "donate maybe." No "I'll think about it." Three piles.

STEP 4: WHEN THE TIMER GOES OFF, STOP.
Even if you're mid-pile. Even if it looks worse than when you started. Stopping on time is what makes it safe to start next time.

STEP 5: DEAL WITH NOT SURE WITHOUT DECIDING.
Box everything up. Write today's date on it. Set a reminder for 30 days. If you haven't needed any of it by then, bin the whole box without opening it.

IF YOU FREEZE: Stop. Stand up. Three slow breaths. Pick up the nearest object. One question only: BIN or NOT SURE? KEEP is the default — if you're frozen, it stays.

IF 10 MINUTES ISN'T ENOUGH: Good. That means it's working. Reset only if you genuinely want to — never because you feel guilty about stopping.

TOMORROW: Pick a different surface. Repeat. You're not finishing the house this week. You're building the habit of starting — and stopping — without the crash.

— SOR7ED
Title: Decluttering for ADHD: Start without spiraling
